sub_problems: How can a small in-context cue trigger the model to re-read the same span of context tokens under a different interpretive frame, producing distinct downstream behavior?
What is the right structural form of the frameshift cue — positional marker, syntactic separator, or learned-token?
How does the system avoid accidental frameshifting on user input that resembles the cue structurally?
What is the limit of frame-multiplicity per context — two, three, n frames per shared content?
